name: Fermented or Brined Pickles
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash cucumbers carefully, using only freshly harvested, slightly immature pickling variety.
2. Weigh the cucumbers and place them in a clean pickling container covered with cold water (cucumbers may be added during the first day or two of curing if enough brine is added to cover them and salt is added in definite amounts to maintain a 10% brine).
3. Add 1 cup salt for each 5 pounds of cucumbers on top of the plate or clean cloth (not directly on the cucumbers) for even distribution throughout the brine.
4. Remove scum when it forms on top of the brine; scum will destroy acidity and result in spoilage if left on.
5. At the end of the week, and for 4 or 5 succeeding weeks, add 1/4 cup salt for each 5 pounds of cucumbers, adding as before.
6. Fermentation resulting in bubble formation should continue about 4 weeks; test by tapping container with your hand and cutting a cucumber in half—if it is the same color throughout and has no noticeable rings or white spots, fermentation is complete.
7. Cucumbers may be kept in this 10% brine solution until made into pickles. Best temperature for brining is about 70 to 75°F.
Note: Use clean stone or glass jars; do not use hard water.
